steps.title: Use the parallel lines condition
steps.content:
Two lines in standard form $Ax + By = C$ are parallel when their coefficients are proportional: $\frac{A_1}{A_2} = \frac{B_1}{B_2}$. For line $j$: $4x + 5y = 55$ and line $k$: $24x + ry = 15$, set up the proportion:
$$\frac{4}{24} = \frac{5}{r}$$

steps.title: Solve for r using cross-multiplication
steps.content:
Simplify the left fraction: $\frac{1}{6} = \frac{5}{r}$. Cross-multiply:
$$1 \cdot r = 6 \cdot 5$$
$$r = 30$$
So line $k$ has equation $24x + 30y = 15$.

steps.title: Apply the point condition to find b
steps.content:
Since line $k$ passes through $(0, b)$, substitute these coordinates into $24x + 30y = 15$:
$$24(0) + 30b = 15$$
$$30b = 15$$
$$b = \frac{15}{30} = \frac{1}{2}$$
Therefore $b = \frac{1}{2}$ or \$0.5$.
